limits._comment: Anti-agentic-trap bounds (research digest §3A). Every agent must respect these; the CLIs read the defaults from here.

human_gates._comment: Automation-bias mitigation: these operations ALWAYS require explicit human approval, regardless of role or autonomy level.
require_human_approval_for: git push or any network publication of workspace content
activating or sending remote webhook notifications (T-005) for the first time
mutating claude.md or gemini.md (harness generation bump via the §5A verification gate)

agents._comment: Capability contracts (intelligent-delegation paper): who can be delegated what. The bench lives in .claude/agents/; gemini-runner is reached through the numbered prompt bridge.

capabilities: decompose goals into blackboard tasks with real depends_on edges
hand off to verifier with evidence
cognitive-diversity checks (verify by a different method than the producer)
grounded answers from papers/ and fetched_docs/ with file+page citations

gemini-runner.bridge: prompts para Gemini/N.md — numbered hand-off files written by the coordinator; the human pastes them into Antigravity and returns the outputs

accepted_mutations.summary: After T-002 (cross-engine contract test) passes, add explicit references in both NLAHs to ORCHESTRATION.md, the blackboard.py/lock.py CLI protocol, TTL/lease semantics, and the producer!=approver rule, replacing the current informal wording.
accepted_mutations.gate: claude.md §5A.4 verification gate + human approval (see state.human_gates)

accepted_mutations.summary: check_lock.py resolves identity only from ambient CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID, so in-session subagents with per-call identity overrides self-block on their own locks and must dodge the hook via Bash writes. Add hc.session_holders() (per-session marker/allowlist file) and allow holders in it.
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: the synthetic PreToolUse payload from T-004 returns exit 0 instead of 2; bash-dodge handoff notes drop to 0
accepted_mutations.gate: code-level (.harness/bin), NOT an NLAH mutation — normal worker task + verifier, no human gate

accepted_mutations.summary: transcript.jsonl held 1 synthetic line vs 77+ real events — the Experience pillar captured no real traffic. Coordinator diagnosis: project-scoped hooks only fire in sessions whose root is THIS repo; today's coordination session ran rooted in another project, so PostToolUse never loaded. Fix: (a) verify transcripts flow in sessions opened in this repo (cheap smoke test), (b) reword the EVERY-tool-call promise to state the session-root condition, (c) optionally document events.jsonl as the engine-agnostic experience floor.
accepted_mutations.evidence: wc -l transcript.jsonl = 1 vs events.jsonl = 77 at audit time; README.md:29-30
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: a session opened in this repo grows transcript.jsonl by >=1 line per tool call; promise wording matches observed behavior
accepted_mutations.gate: doc/NLAH wording parts bundle into human-gated T-007; hook smoke test is code-level
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-007 (promise wording: gemini.md §4A, README, ORCHESTRATION §3); residual: hook smoke test in a repo-rooted session

accepted_mutations.summary: recontext_evidence.md is the only many-writer shared mutable file with no lock rule and no guarded() serialization (gemini-runner wrote it with zero locks). Add a lock-before-write rule to gemini.md §4B and the file header; optionally ship recontext.py add for atomic appends.
accepted_mutations.evidence: grep -c recontext_evidence events.jsonl = 0; gemini.md:62-67 has no lock step
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: recontext lock events > 0 before writes; two-writer stress test yields zero interleaved/truncated entries
accepted_mutations.gate: gemini.md edit is human-gated (bundle into T-007); CLI part is code-level
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-007 (gemini.md §4B lock rule) + T-009 (recontext.py atomic CLI)

accepted_mutations.summary: lock_is_expired compares floored acquired_at (ISO_FMT, second precision) against full-microsecond now_utc(), so a --ttl 1 lock acquired at X.999 can expire ~1ms later. Fix: floor both sides (now_utc().replace(microsecond=0)) so effective lifetime is [ttl, ttl+1).
accepted_mutations.evidence: harness_common.py:30/:38/:147; repro acquire@X.999 --ttl 1 -> effective_life 0.001s; T-010.json 2x 'per G6' TTL-override notes
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: repro yields effective_life >= 1.0s; 'per G6' TTL-override notes drop to 0 in future contention smokes
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-013, verified done by harness-verifier (all measurable gains confirmed TRUE, combined regression passed)

accepted_mutations.summary: Busy refusals (lock contention) return without any log_event, so contention is invisible to the 5A evolution loop. Add a lock_busy event (with normalized path per P-008) on every busy-refusal branch.
accepted_mutations.evidence: lock.py:50-60 and recontext.py:68-78 return with no log_event; grep -c lock_busy events.jsonl = 0
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: grep -c lock_busy events.jsonl > 0 after a contention smoke; busy-refusals-per-task becomes computable
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-014, verified done by harness-verifier (all measurable gains confirmed TRUE, combined regression passed)

accepted_mutations.summary: update --artifact uses argparse store, so repeated flags in one call silently drop all but the last. Change to action=append and extend the artifact list.
accepted_mutations.evidence: blackboard.py:407 add_argument('--artifact', default=None); cmd_update appends only args.artifact
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: update --artifact A --artifact B records both A and B
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-015, verified done by harness-verifier (all measurable gains confirmed TRUE, combined regression passed)

accepted_mutations.summary: Path normalization missing at the event-log boundary: lock.py logs args.path verbatim (abs leaks), recontext logs hardcoded absolute EVIDENCE_PATH, notify logs str(CONFIG_PATH). Fix option (a): inline-normalize at each log site via Path(p).resolve().relative_to(ROOT) with fallback to the original string for out-of-repo paths.
accepted_mutations.evidence: 9x '/Users/' in events.jsonl: 4 recontext (ev116/117/160/161, recontext.py:47), 1 notify (ev199, notify.py:200), 4 lock.py caller-supplied (ev226-229, lock.py:66/:98)
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: lock.py acquire <abs-in-repo-path> logs relative path; grep '/Users/' over newly appended events = 0
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-014, verified done by harness-verifier (all measurable gains confirmed TRUE, combined regression passed)

accepted_mutations.source: gen-3 early item (T-091 slip residuals input) prioritized by the human 2026-07-04
accepted_mutations.summary: Mechanical producer!=approver: update --status done refused unless task is in review AND acting agent != handoff.from; --override-producer-check requires --note and logs producer_check_overridden; task_updated events carry an explicit agent field (closes F7 for status changes).
accepted_mutations.evidence: T-091 slip (self-done from in_progress via ambient identity); verifier-b attack table 14/14 incl. the exact slip vector (omit --agent) now refused
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: self-done from any non-review status = exit 1 (was exit 0); done-by-producer in review = exit 1; ambient-identity actor now explicit in events.jsonl
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-031 (substrate-worker-1), verdicted by verifier-b (rotated reviewer, F6 practice). INFO semantics note: guardrail checks producer-OF-RECORD (handoff.from), not lifetime authorship - a re-handoff by a second agent legitimately re-opens the original producer as an eligible approver

accepted_mutations.source: HUMAN-DIRECTED (operator, 2026-07-05): Fable 5 access ends 2026-07-07; harness must be model-agnostic. Gate satisfied by explicit operator instruction; audit-exempt
accepted_mutations.summary: De-Fable: every normative reference to Fable 5 reworded to 'the Coordinator' = strongest Claude model available in the main session (tested with Fable 5; runs identically on Opus or Sonnet). Framework renamed 'Universal Agent Harness Orchestration Framework'. Rewording only - roles, gates, limits, protocols unchanged.
accepted_mutations.evidence: Pre-change: normative Fable refs in claude.md title/HarnessCard/section5, gemini.md, ORCHESTRATION.md section4, README. Post-change grep: only historical parentheticals remain. Machine layer had ZERO Fable deps (bin/ clean, bench agents pin opus/sonnet).
accepted_mutations.measurable_gain: grep -rni fable over the 5 normative docs returns 0 requirement-implying mentions; harness runs identically when the main session model is Opus/Sonnet after 2026-07-07
accepted_mutations.gate: NLAH mutation - human gate SATISFIED by direct operator instruction 2026-07-05
accepted_mutations.applied_note: applied in T-034 (substrate-worker-1), verified by verifier-b (hunk-by-hunk drift hunt: zero semantic changes; active_role correctly left for the gen-3 bundle)

evolution.notes: Gen-2 audit (T-012) accepted on rev-2 after one REJECTED round (P-008 evidence error, self-disclosed in audit 2.4 - producer!=approver worked). Worker partition: WA=T-013 (P-005: harness_common.py+T-010.json+README.md), WB=T-014 (P-006+P-008a: lock.py+recontext.py+notify.py), WC=T-015 (P-007: blackboard.py). Disjoint; option (b) shared helper rejected to preserve the split. NO NLAH mutation this generation - no human gate required (verified twice). Generation 2 closed 2026-07-04T07:35Z: P-005..P-008 applied via T-013/T-014/T-015, all verified. No NLAH edit => no human gate, but generation bumped because the control plane semantics changed (TTL floor, lock_busy events, path normalization, artifact append).
next_audit_inputs: mdtoc friction: tests/__init__.py needed by py3.9 unittest discover was unowned by the T-020 plan; two workers hit it, worker-2 created it as disclosed shared bootstrap. Planner checklist candidate: enumerate shared bootstrap files (test package markers, conftest-like infra) as owned artifacts.
Nit (T-013 verify): mechanically-exact TTL interval is (ttl, ttl+1], README says [ttl, ttl+1) - prose is correct, bracket notation imprecise at measure-zero boundaries
Shape change (T-015): task_updated events now log artifact as a list (was string) - downstream log parsers must handle both

Race (fix-verify session): verifier's claim-refusal probe on T-027 accidentally SUCCEEDED when deps went done mid-test; it self-healed by releasing. Candidate guardrail: a probe-claim convention (claim with --dry-run?) or use permanently-gated scratch tasks for refusal probes
F1 (T-021/T-022): tests/__init__.py unowned bootstrap - py3.9 unittest discover needs it when -s != -t (bpo-23882). Planner checklist: enumerate shared bootstrap files as owned artifacts

F2+F8 (T-028): check hardcodes DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH=3, no --max-depth flag, and generate persists no depth record -> structural false-stale when generated at non-default depth. Fix needs stored config/frontmatter or a check flag

F3 (T-027): double-claim race - verifier probe-claim on a live open task succeeded when deps went done mid-test; claim != safe read. Guardrail candidate: --dry-run claim or permanently-gated scratch tasks for refusal probes
F4 (T-027): naive base->count dedup contract mandates output collision ['foo','foo-1','foo-1'] vs real github-slugger recursion - contract revision + new golden vectors is a real gen-3 item
F5 (T-027): slugger_c's cited rule-table doc style is an uplift candidate for the promoted slugger_b
F6 (epic mdtoc): verdict-layer monoculture - harness-verifier was sole approver of all 9 producer tasks and authored the join; consider verifier rotation or second reviewer on epic joins

F7 (events.jsonl): task_updated status=done events carry NO actor field; top-level agent is uniformly the ambient session id - producer!=approver auditing is reconstruction-dependent. Add explicit actor to verdict events

P-009 follow-ups: (i) done-on-done now refused (idempotence loss, coherent but note it); (ii) F7 remains open for events other than task_updated; (iii) consider lifetime-authorship mode if producer-of-record proves too lax in practice

U1 (Thariq unknowns, pre): planner contract - plan.md must carry an Unknowns section per epic (4 quadrants); blocking known-unknowns resolved with the human or converted to spike tasks BEFORE publishing the DAG. Target: .claude/agents/orchestration-planner.md + plan.md template (code-level, no NLAH gate)

U2 (Thariq unknowns, during): note taxonomy DECISION/DEVIATION/TRADEOFF/OPEN-QUESTION as a convention (optionally blackboard.py --note-kind flag) so audits can count and chase OPEN-QUESTIONs mechanically

U3 (Thariq unknowns, pre): blindspot-pass step - before dispatching a new epic, a thinker interviews the human with concrete assumptions to surface unknown-knowns (interview pattern); coordinator uses AskUserQuestion

U4 (Thariq unknowns, post): before heavyweight human gates (first git push), the epic join must produce an explainer + 3 comprehension questions for the human - aligns with operator rule 'no publicar lo que no se entiende' (2026-07-04)

NLAH-GATED (needs human approval): CLAUDE.md 2B instructs agents to read .harness/active_role, but no CLI/hook reads it and migration does not seed it (verifier-c T-032). Reconcile: drop the stale reference or seed a template. Awaiting operator decision

P-009 friction (confirmed twice, T-032+T-033 verifies): ANY verifier action that moves a review task off 'review' (claiming it, or setting in_progress) locks the verifier out of the done path and forces --override-producer-check even when actor != producer. Refinement: allow done whenever a handoff exists and actor != handoff.from regardless of current status, or make claim preserve review status for verifier-role claims
